description: Draws a rectangular annotation defined by four anchor points. Creates a rectangle connecting four specified points with optional styling.
utility: Highlight or annotate rectangular regions in diagrams with four-point anchors

arguments.name: point1
arguments.description: First anchor point, specified as at(row, col)

arguments.name: point2
arguments.description: Second anchor point, specified as at(row, col)

arguments.name: point3
arguments.description: Third anchor point, specified as at(row, col)

arguments.name: point4
arguments.description: Fourth anchor point, specified as at(row, col)

arguments.name: style
arguments.description: Optional styling modifiers (c for color, s() for style, dash, fadeout, etc.)

alternates.syntax: annotationrect(at(r1, c1), at(r2, c2), at(r3, c3), at(r4, c4), c(green))

examples.scenario: Highlight standard form equation box with lightblue fill (equation of a circle lesson)
Highlight intermediate step with orange stroke and timing (axis intercepts lesson)
Highlight final answer with default styling (circle from diameter lesson)
Highlight lightblue result box (axis intercepts lesson)
Deferred annotation rect for later reveal (circle from diameter lesson)
